description: Can you reproduce the bug with the given steps?
Can you narrow down to the specific file/function?
Did git log reveal related recent changes?
Does the error message/stack trace point to root cause?
Is a null/undefined value being accessed?
Is there a wrong condition, comparison, or calculation?
Are async operations racing or executing out of order?
Is a value a different type than expected?
Is an edge case or boundary condition not handled?
Is a configuration value wrong or missing?
Did a dependency update break something?
Is the input data malformed or unexpected?
Is this the smallest possible fix for the root cause?
Does the fix avoid breaking other functionality?
Is there a test that catches this exact bug?
Were similar patterns checked elsewhere in codebase?
Is the fix documented (memory saved, comments added)?
